framing: The reason these bugs survive to production is that they don't show up unless you test against the actual shape Stripe sends — a payload you typed from memory won't have the null customer_email, so your test passes and production doesn't.

kit_checks.name: fire
kit_checks.line: your handler ACCEPTS a correctly-signed real event (HTTP 2xx)

kit_checks.name: fire --forge
kit_checks.line: your handler REJECTS a forged/badly-signed event (HTTP 4xx)

kit_checks.name: check-email
kit_checks.line: buyer email is read from customer_details.email

kit_limits: does not talk to the real Stripe API
does not validate your business logic
not a substitute for stripe listen

gotchas.title: customer_email is null on checkout.session.completed
gotchas.symptom: On a normal Checkout completion the top-level customer_email is null; the buyer's address is at customer_details.email. Reading only customer_email silently drops the sale — the payment goes through, the customer is charged, and the handler has no idea who to deliver to.
gotchas.why: Stripe Checkout does not put the buyer's email in the top-level customer_email field on a normal completion. It collects the email during checkout and puts it in customer_details.email. The top-level customer_email is only populated when you set it yourself when creating the session (pre-filled from a logged-in user), or on some older/legacy integrations.
gotchas.fix: Read customer_details.email first, fall back to customer_email (the pre-filled/legacy case).

gotchas.title: success_url only expands {CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}
gotchas.symptom: Any other placeholder (e.g. {CHECKOUT_EMAIL}) is passed through literally — the buyer's browser lands on a URL with a raw {CHECKOUT_EMAIL} in it. ({CHECKOUT_EMAIL} is not a real placeholder.)
gotchas.fix: Use only {CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}, then resolve the buyer server-side from the session id.

gotchas.title: Verify the Stripe-Signature header
gotchas.symptom: A handler that skips verification accepts forged events from anyone who knows the endpoint URL.
gotchas.why: The signature is HMAC-SHA256 over "{t}." + raw_body, keyed by your whsec_ secret.
gotchas.fix: Recompute the HMAC, constant-time compare against each v1=, reject on mismatch.

gotchas.title: Enforce the 300-second timestamp tolerance
gotchas.symptom: Without it, a captured request can be replayed indefinitely.
gotchas.fix: Reject events whose t= timestamp is more than 300s from now.

gotchas.title: Sign/verify over the RAW request bytes
gotchas.symptom: Re-serialising the JSON (different key order or whitespace) changes the bytes and breaks the signature.
gotchas.fix: Verify against the exact body received; do not parse-then-reserialise before verifying.

gotchas.title: Return 2xx fast, and process idempotently
gotchas.symptom: Stripe retries on any non-2xx, so slow or failing handlers get the same event again.
gotchas.fix: Acknowledge quickly, dedupe on event.id, and do heavy work out-of-band.
